description: Collaborative Whiteboard MVP - A hand-drawn style online whiteboard similar to Excalidraw, with basic drawing tools, element manipulation, and local persistence. MVP focuses on single-user functionality.

userStories.title: Initialize React project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind project scaffolded so I can build the whiteboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts` in project root
Install and configure Tailwind CSS
Remove default boilerplate, add a blank white canvas area
npm run dev starts successfully, page renders blank white canvas
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: Define element data types and store
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript interfaces and a simple state store for canvas elements so other features can reference them.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Element interface with: id, type (freehand|rectangle|arrow|text), x, y, width, height, points (for freehand), text (for text), strokeColor, strokeWidth, backgroundColor
Create a simple store (React Context or Zustand) that holds an array of elements
Export store and types from a single entry point
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement canvas renderer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTML5 Canvas component that renders elements from the store so I can see drawings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Canvas component using HTML5 Canvas API
Render rectangle elements as filled/outlined rects
Render freehand elements as connected line segments
Re-render on store changes (useEffect on elements array)
Canvas fills the viewport
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add toolbar with tool selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want a toolbar with drawing tool buttons so I can choose what to draw.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixed toolbar at the top or left of screen
Buttons for: Select, Freehand, Rectangle, Arrow, Text
Active tool highlighted visually
Store current tool in store state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement freehand drawing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw freehand lines on the canvas so I can sketch ideas.
acceptanceCriteria: mousedown+mousemove+mouseup creates a freehand element with recorded points
Lines render with hand-drawn style (slight wobble or smooth curves)
Default stroke color: black, width: 2
Element added to store on mouseup
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement rectangle drawing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw rectangles on the canvas so I can create boxes and containers.
acceptanceCriteria: mousedown+mousemove shows live rectangle preview
mouseup creates rectangle element with correct x, y, width, height
Rectangle renders with outline (no fill by default)
Element added to store on mouseup
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement arrow/line drawing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw arrows between points so I can show relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: mousedown+mousemove shows live line preview with arrowhead
mouseup creates arrow element with start and end coordinates
Arrow renders as line with arrowhead at end
Element added to store on mouseup
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement text element creation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add text labels to the canvas so I can annotate my drawings.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking canvas with Text tool shows a text input overlay
User types text and presses Enter or clicks outside to confirm
Creates text element at click position
Default font: sans-serif, 16px, black
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add element selection and movement
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select and drag elements so I can reposition them.
acceptanceCriteria: Select tool: clicking an element shows selection handles (outline + 4 corner handles)
Drag selected element to reposition it
Click empty area deselects
Only one element selected at a time
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add element deletion
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete selected elements so I can remove mistakes.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Delete or Backspace key removes selected element from store
Pressing Escape deselects without deleting
No error when no element is selected
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add stroke color and width options
userStories.description: As a user, I want to change stroke color and width so I can make my drawings visually distinct.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar shows color picker (default palette: 6 colors) and stroke width selector (1, 2, 3, 4)
Changing color/width updates current tool settings
New elements use the selected color and width
Does not affect existing elements
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add undo/redo support
userStories.description: As a user, I want to undo and redo actions so I can fix mistakes.
acceptanceCriteria: Track history of store states (action-based: add/modify/delete)
Ctrl+Z undoes last action, Ctrl+Shift+Z redoes
Undo/redo buttons in toolbar
Max history: 50 states
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add localStorage persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want my whiteboard to persist between sessions so I don't lose work.
acceptanceCriteria: Save elements array to localStorage on every store change (debounced 500ms)
Load from localStorage on page load
Handle empty/invalid localStorage gracefully (start with empty canvas)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add export as PNG image
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export my whiteboard as a PNG image so I can share it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Export button to toolbar
Clicking renders canvas to a clean PNG (white background, all elements included)
Triggers browser download of PNG file
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
